name: lifecycle-triage
description: Classify a lifecycle request before choosing direct patch, review, spec, or closure workflow.

arguments.name: request_summary
arguments.description: Short summary of the user's requested work.

arguments.name: repo_root
arguments.description: Repository root. Defaults to the current workspace when omitted.

instructions: Classify the request as trivial, small, spec-needed, review, or closure.
Use repository governance, active spec state, durable docs, and risk level to justify the classification.

For spec-needed work with broad durable-doc impact, stale-doc risk, imported durable sources, or resumed-spec drift, inspect the concrete authority risk before implementation readiness; recommend canonical-context.md or an explicit canonical-context import plan when useful, but keep the diagnostic advisory unless repository policy makes it blocking.

When a repository-evidence provider returns sparse, low-signal, stale, truncated, or surprising routing output, treat it as inconclusive routing evidence. Pivot to repository instructions, durable indexes, targeted file inventory, and direct reads before making absence, drift, implementation, or root-cause claims.
Route trivial and small work to proportionate validation; route behavior, cross-module, packaging, governance, or ambiguous work to an active or new spec.
client_support_recovery: Use MCP scan_specs and active_spec_preflight when available. If MCP tools are unavailable or repo-evidence search is sparse, inspect AGENTS.md, docs/README.md, docs/specs, docs/backlog, docs/roadmap, and targeted source inventories before classifying.
